heading: Required coverage for reconstructive surgery following mastectomies
label: 300gg–27 Required coverage for reconstructive surgery following mastectomies
text: The provisions of shall apply to group health plans, and and health insurance issuers offering group or individual health insurance coverage, as if included in this subpart.section 1185b of title 291So in original.1
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to renumbering by .section 300gg–6 of this titlePub. L. 111–148

Amendments2010—, formerly § 1562(c)(5), as renumbered by , substituted “and health insurance issuers offering group or individual health insurance coverage” for “health insurance issuers providing health insurance coverage in connection with group health plans”.Pub. L. 111–148, § 1563(c)(5)Pub. L. 111–148, § 10107(b)(1)

Effective Date, , , 2681–438, provided that:Pub. L. 105–277, div. A, § 101(f) [title IX, § 903(c)(1)]112 Stat. 2681–337Oct. 21, 1998“(A).—In generalThe amendment made by subsection (a) [enacting this section] shall apply to group health plans for plan years beginning on or after the date of enactment of this Act [].Oct. 21, 1998“(B).—Special rule for collective bargaining agreementsIn the case of a group health plan maintained pursuant to 1 or more collective bargaining agreements between employee representatives and 1 or more employers, any plan amendment made pursuant to a collective bargaining agreement relating to the plan which amends the plan solely to conform to any requirement added by the amendment made by subsection (a) shall not be treated as a termination of such collective bargaining agreement.”
